pages.raw_text:
" A warm session Of the trades
council was held last Evening ut
the union hall. The main topic Of
discussion was the trouble now ex-
listing between the master bakers
and the Quaker Bread company.
. The story of the trouble is given
as follows by prominent member
of the trades council:


The Master Bakers association
succeeded in getting the bakers
union to pass a resolution that the
members of that union would not
supply men for bakers who did not
belong to the Master Bakers asso-
citation.


Then the next move of the master
bakers was to adopt a resolution
not to wrap the bread turned out
by their association.


The Quaker Bread company re-
fused to come into the association
under this clause and the members
of the bakers union were conf
PEELED under the resolution passed
to stop work for the Quaker people,
despite the fact that Mr. Harper,
the manager, had agreed to pay the
union wages.


The result of the meeting Of the
council last night was to rescind
its former action of supporting the
master bakers and it will leave the
matter to be fought out by Mr..
Harper and the bakers union. It
| is a well-known fact that the re
tail grocers are behind the Quaker
concern. The fact that the Quaker
Bread company would not enter the
association was all that was neces.
sary for the bakers union to call
out it's members from that estab
liShment..
